title: Ixora longshanensis
intro: Ixora longshanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the jungle flame or firecracker plant, native to the tropical regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ixora longshanensis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ixora longshanensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Ixora longshanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ixora, which includes approximately 400 species. The genus Ixora is part of the large and diverse family Rubiaceae, which contains over 13,000 species. The classification of Ixora longshanensis is further divided into the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
